items.title: Creditors of NT mine with $360m debt criticise lack of oversight

children.content: In the wake of Nathan River Resources entering into voluntary administration, creditors have criticised the NT government for its lack of oversight of the struggling company. ASIC documents show the mining company owes more than $360 million.

articles.title: Iron ore company 'undermines' promises after failing to pay traditional owners

children.content: Nathan River Resources went into administration and owes about $360 million to creditors including the NT government and traditional owners.

image.altText: A digger is filling up a truck with iron ore in the NT.

gallery.altText: A man standing with his arms folded, in front of several long metal pipes in an outdoor work yard.

thumbnail.altText: A man standing with his arms folded, in front of several long metal pipes in an outdoor work yard.

avatar.alt: Jane Bardon in a blazer looking at the camera.

items.title: NT government axes controversial $1.1 million private midwife contract

children.content: The Northern Territory government has axed a $1.1 million contract awarded to a Darwin midwife, banned from working unsupervised, almost 10 months after doctors began raising concerns about the deal.

image.altText: A woman in a brown dress looks at the camera.

items.title: Man sentenced to 10 years in jail for stabbing death of niece in Darwin

children.content: Andrew Mungatopi has been sentenced to 10 years and two months in prison for the fatal stabbing of his niece in a Darwin backyard in 2024.

image.altText: A First Nations man wearing mirrored sunglasses, pointing at himself.

items.title: NT attorney-general backs community safety changes despite unrest

children.content: The government says bail law changes made a year ago are working despite a string of tragedies and unrest in bush communities.

gallery.altText: Close-up of a white woman, blonde hair, wearing a light blue blazer, white blouse, looking intently at camera, soft smile.

thumbnail.altText: Close-up of a white woman, blonde hair, wearing a light blue blazer, white blouse, looking intently at camera, soft smile.

items.title: Three hospitalised as Central Australian family dispute turns violent

children.content: Three people have been hospitalised after a family dispute at a Central Australian outstation turned violent over the weekend, sparking fears of further unrest.

image.altText: Two women, looking serious, sitting on chairs inside a studio.

items.title: 'National reluctance' to discuss abuse of Indigenous children, says ex-minister

children.content: A review has begun into the family circumstances of Kumanjayi Little Baby leading up to her disappearance and death, which relatives hope will find answers.

image.altText: An Indigenous woman with grey curly hair wearing a black top sitting in a living room.

items.title: Police release body-worn footage of fugitive bikie's dramatic arrest

children.content: Northern Territory Police have released dramatic body-worn footage of the arrest of Mongols bikie Brandt Graham after the 44-year-old jumped a court dock last week, sparking a five-day manhunt.

image.altText: Nold_GrahamArrest_1006

articles.title: Mongols fugitive taken down by dog squad after fleeing traffic stop on foot

children.content: Police have arrested fugitive Top End Mongols bikie Brandt Graham five days after the 44-year-old escaped from custody last week, sparking an "extensive manhunt".

image.altText: A shirtless man in jeans with his face blurred is surrounded by three police officers who hold his arms

avatar.alt: A journalist wearing a button-up shirt, with a neutral facial expression.

items.title: Inpex seeks urgent orders to stop strike action in Darwin

children.content: Oil and gas giant Inpex has launched proceedings with the Fair Work Commission seeking urgent orders to stop strike action at its Ichthys LNG facility in Darwin.

image.altText: Inpex shipment

items.title: Mining set to restart at one of the NT's biggest gold reserves

children.content: After being dormant for nearly 30 years, the Mount Todd gold mine near Katherine is gearing up to restart production. While there are calls for local Aboriginal people to have the "first crack" at mining jobs, the operator is planning to engage a largely FIFO workforce.

image.altText: A miner inspects a large rock.

items.title: First Indigenous SANFL player David Kantilla inducted into hall of fame

children.content: Six new members are inducted into the Australian Football Hall of Fame, including Geelong and Gold Coast legend Gary Ablett Jr.

image.altText: Gary Ablett Jr in a tuxedo seen side-on with his name on a screen behind him.

items.title: Is Hanson running One Nation or taking orders from Gina Rinehart?

children.content: Federal Treasurer Jim Chalmers addresses One Nation's surge in popularity, saying Pauline Hanson's party is capitalising on Australians feeling disconnected due to economic pressure.

"The pace of change in our economy and in our society is accelerating and the global pressures are intensifying, and I think people have got legitimate concerns about where they fit in that story," Mr Chalmers tells 7.30.

image.altText: Treasurer Jim Chalmers in a navy suit and maroon tie, pressing his lips tightly.

gallery.altText: Treasurer Jim Chalmers in a navy suit and maroon tie, pressing his lips tightly.

thumbnail.altText: Treasurer Jim Chalmers in a navy suit and maroon tie, pressing his lips tightly.

items.title: NDIS inquiry told 240,000 people to be 'exited' from scheme

children.content: A Senate inquiry into the government's National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS) changes has been told 240,000 people will be "exited" from the scheme. Follow live.

image.altText: A man in a navy suit and tie looks sternly into camera.

items.title: Air India crash plunged victims' families 'into darkness'

children.content: One year on from the fatal Air India crash that claimed 260 lives, families of victims are still waiting for a report into the investigation.

image.altText: A schreenshot of a couple from a video call.

items.title: Couple's $3,000 wedding 'so much fun' as more Australians choose micro-events

children.content: For just $3,000, compared to the average wedding cost of about $38,000, Melbourne couple Averill and Lloyd Johnson pulled off their dream wedding.

gallery.altText: Groom wearing a suit and top hat and bridge wearing a black dress, holding up their middle fingers with rings on them.

thumbnail.altText: Groom wearing a suit and top hat and bridge wearing a black dress, holding up their middle fingers with rings on them.

items.title: Amex ordered to compensate man whose ex accessed his data

children.content: Australia's privacy commissioner has threatened a complainant with legal action to prevent the full disclosure of the findings of a long-running investigation into American Express's information security, which found widespread technology failures.

image.altText: A modern office building with an American Express sign.

items.title: How you and your superannuation came to be aboard SpaceX

children.content: If your superannuation is managed by any of Australia's big superannuation funds, chances are you're about to become a highly valued financial backer to Elon Musk.

image.altText: smoke and flames emerge from a spacex launch in texas

items.title: Century-old Venus flytrap mystery solved, scientists claim

image.altText: Two Venus flytraps against a black background, one on the left is open, the one on the right is shut.

children.content: Physicists think they have found the cellular mechanism behind a Venus flytrap's signature move, but plant scientists say the mystery isn't solved yet.

items.title: US forecasters say potentially record-breaking El Niño underway

children.content: There is a high chance this year's Pacific climate phenomenon will be among the strongest on record, which could lead to hotter temperatures across the globe.

image.altText: Storm clouds drop heavy rain in the distance on a western Queensland property

items.title: The Australian connection behind Roxane Gay and Channing Tatum's novel

children.content: It's possible the ball may not have got rolling on Roxane Gay and Channing Tatum's upcoming novel if it wasn't for an Australian connection. Gay will appear at Vivid Sydney in a wide-ranging discussion.

image.altText: Roxane Gay with hair in black and grey braids tied in a bun looking out a window.

items.title: Friday news quiz: Fake weather, a whale graveyard and James Bond going woke

children.content: Don't let your confidence be shaken or stirred by this week's test of how much you've been paying attention.

gallery.altText: Daniel Craig, as James Bond, stands next to his Aston Martin in a scene from Skyfall.

thumbnail.altText: Daniel Craig, as James Bond, stands next to his Aston Martin in a scene from Skyfall.

items.title: Hundreds of 'extraordinary' Cartier jewellery pieces on display at NGV

children.content: Glittering tiaras and dazzling necklaces worn by Hollywood icons such as Elizabeth Taylor and Grace Kelly are featured in an exhibition at the National Gallery of Victoria in Melbourne.

image.altText: A silver Cartier tiara from 1914

items.title: Driver spends $160 rego rebate on pothole-blown tyre

children.content: Motorists in Victoria's south-west say they are spending their car rego rebate on blown tyres following damage caused by potholes.

image.altText: a man in a navy jumper stands in front of a tyre

items.title: Three decades later, 18 soldiers lost in a training crash are not forgotten

children.content: During counterterrorism training in 1996, two Black Hawk helicopters collided. Eighteen men were killed, making it Australia's worst peacetime military aviation disaster.

gallery.altText: Graphic showing a helicopter, crash remnants, and tributes

thumbnail.altText: Graphic showing a helicopter, crash remnants, and tributes

items.title: Study reveals complex links between social media use and teen wellbeing

children.content: A decade-long study following almost 1,200 Melbourne school students finds a "small increased risk" of depressive symptoms for teenagers using social media at least two hours a day.

image.altText: teens using their phones with various social media related illustrations

items.title: ASX set for strong start after Wall Street rebounds

children.content: The ASX is set for a strong start after Wall Street rallied and oil prices tumbled. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: ASX

items.title: Sydney woman's suspected remains found eight years after alleged murder

children.content: Nadire Sensoy disappeared from Sydney's west in 2018, with her disappearance triggering a police reward and coronial inquest.

image.altText: A woman smiling and wearing a blue shirt.

items.title: Man charged over Sydney woman's alleged kidnapping murder

children.content: Family members told police they last heard from the woman on Monday, when she told them she was visiting a relative in Sydney.

image.altText: Police tape at a crime scene

items.title: Lauren can play like a 'normal kid' thanks to 600,000 strangers

children.content: Lauren Zeller has a one-in-a-million bleeding disorder, but strangers' blood donations give her a chance to play like other kids.

gallery.altText: little girl runs by a pool

thumbnail.altText: little girl runs by a pool

items.title: Major PFAS class action could lead to multi-billion dollar claim figure

children.content: Hundreds of businesses and organisations are part of a major Victorian Supreme Court class action against chemical manufacturer 3M.

image.altText: An industrial plant in the Melbourne suburb of Altona.

items.title: International students face a double financial blow

children.content: When Jolie Cao chose to do her undergraduate university degree in Australia, she knew it would be expensive. But an additional financial pressure has made things "really hard".

image.altText: A woman with black hair in a puffer jacket smiling to camera.

items.title: Culture of silence: Whistleblowers react to NSW Police review

children.content: Former NSW Police officers who blew the whistle on the toxic culture of the force have welcomed an independent report "validating" their experiences.

image.altText: Mel wearing a white jacket and black top

items.title: Canberra's only water park is up for sale. Here's why that's good news

children.content: The site will not undergo redevelopment or demolition before its sale and locals say it is the result they fought for.

image.altText: Three tower waterslides, all in a significant state of disrepair, surrounded by gum trees.

items.title: Number of dogs euthanased on Sunshine Coast triples after law change

children.content: The number of dogs euthanased by a regional Queensland council has more than tripled in recent years, with more dogs put down there than in Brisbane, Australia's largest council by population size.

gallery.altText: Sarge looks through a wire gate, looks sad.

thumbnail.altText: Sarge looks through a wire gate, looks sad.

items.title: Celine Cremer search team member to assist with Nannup Four case

children.content: The vice-president of a search team that solved two high-profile missing persons cases in Tasmania turns his attention to the Nannup Four case after it was featured in the Expanse podcast.

image.altText: A photo of two men, a woman and a girl with a photo of bushland in the background.

items.title: 'Insane' spike in thefts forces roadside honesty stalls to close

children.content: Mandarin grower Bruce Crain says people's growing dishonesty, including the theft of 70 bags in one weekend, makes it impossible to offer a roadside service.

image.altText: Several bags of mandarins on a black table with a sign reading "$5 per bag" placed inside of a corrugated steel shelter.
